test.details: All 13 packages passed: auth, events, gateway, provider, proxy, runtime, sandbox, server, store, types, vmctl, vmmanager

lint.details: No lint script configured in package.json - considered N/A

featuresReviewed.summary: Work registry foundation with WorkItem type, work_items table, 6 CRUD store methods, 20 comprehensive tests covering state transitions and parent-child relationships
POST /api/agent/spawn endpoint with parent-child task linking, 16 tests covering auth, validation, error handling, and registry integration
GET /api/agent/:id/status endpoint with full task record retrieval, 9 tests covering auth gating, caller scoping, 404 handling, and state transitions
Channel-based parent-child communication with 22 tests covering message delivery, result collection, scoping, concurrent access, and auto-channel creation
Research button in etext editor with UI state management, progress indicator, results panel, 9 Playwright tests covering full user flow
Concurrent worker support with auto-notification, work item sync, 16 tests including race detection, timing verification, and stress tests
Worker failure isolation with cancellation, error propagation, parent resilience, 24 tests covering all failure scenarios and recovery

nonBlockingIssues.description: Feature spec mentions 403 for cross-user tasks, but implementation returns 404. This is intentional and consistent with existing handlers to prevent IDOR probing.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the authorization pattern of returning 404 (not 403) for cross-user resource access to prevent IDOR probing. Both HandleTaskStatus and HandleTaskStatusByID use this pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scheduler-status-api review found this pattern used consistently across runtime API handlers but not documented in AGENTS.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the URL path parameter extraction pattern used in runtime API handlers (TrimPrefix/TrimSuffix) as an alternative to query parameters.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scheduler-status-api review found HandleTaskStatusByID uses manual path parsing which differs from query-parameter approach

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the context persistence pattern for shutdown scenarios: when a task's context is cancelled, persistence operations must use context.Background() to ensure state is saved.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scheduler-worker-failure-isolation review identified this critical pattern in handleExecutionError at runtime.go:726-758

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that runtime API endpoints are hosted within the sandbox service (port 8085) rather than as a standalone service.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scheduler-status-api review noted this could be clearer since runtime API routes are part of the public API surface

sharedStateHighlights.description: All workers correctly followed the ErrNotFound pattern from store package and reused TaskState type for consistency
All workers followed TDD pattern as documented in go-worker SKILL.md - wrote tests first, then implementation
Comprehensive library documentation created by all workers covering schema, API details, patterns, and downstream dependencies
Consistent use of X-Authenticated-User header for auth context across runtime API handlers
Parent-child channel communication properly extends existing ChannelManager infrastructure rather than duplicating
Strong test patterns established: testConcurrentSetup helper, conditionalFailProvider for failure injection, timing-based concurrency verification
